title: [synth] Lower Variadic Crash
body:
@uenoku - when trying to lower this simple example I'm getting errors that surprise me as the example is very close to one of the tests which does pass... Running `circt-opt --synth-lower-variadic`


```mlir
module {
  hw.module @sop_three(in %a : i16, in %b : i16, in %c : i16, in %d : i16, out product : i16) {
    %0 = comb.mul %a, %b, %c : i16
    hw.output %0 : i16
  }
}
```

Error generated - looks like it is to do with the lowering pipeline performed during timing analysis (with timing-aware=false) it passes:
```
Visiting:  %0 = comb.mul %a, %b : i16[0]
Visiting default:  %0 = comb.mul %a, %b : i16[0]
[pass-manager Pass.cpp:1030 1] Starting PassManager run on operation 'module {...}' with 1 passes, verifyPasses=1 pipeline: any(hw.module(hw-aggregate-to-comb,convert-comb-to-synth{ max-emulation-unknown-bits=10 target-ir=aig},cse,canonicalize{  max-iterations=10 max-num-rewrites=-1 region-simplify=normal test-convergence=false top-down=true}))
[pass-manager Pass.cpp:1046 2] Registering dependent dialects for pipeline
[pass-manager Pass.cpp:1051 2] Loading dialect: builtin
[pass-manager Pass.cpp:1051 2] Loading dialect: comb
[pass-manager Pass.cpp:1051 2] Loading dialect: synth
[mlircontext MLIRContext.cpp:477 1] Load new dialect in Context synth
LLVM ERROR: Loading a dialect (synth) while in a multi-threaded execution context (maybe the PassManager): this can indicate a missing `dependentDialects` in a pass for example.
```
code_blocks:

  hw.module @sop_three(in %a : i16, in %b : i16, in %c : i16, in %d : i16, out product : i16) {
